title: Pisophaca proxima
uses: Pisophaca proxima, also known as Purple Sage, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. It has also been used to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its supposed anti-emetic properties. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat fever and respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pisophaca proxima for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Pisophaca proxima, also known as Mexican gold poppy, is believed to have various health benefits. Psoralea corylifolia, also known as Babchi or Psoralea proxima is not found,  is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurveda and Unani. The health benefits of Psoralea corylifolia include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and photoprotective properties, which may help to protect against skin damage and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Psoralea corylifolia include psoralen, isopsoralen, and bergapten, which are responsible for its phototoxic effects and potential therapeutic benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Psoralea corylifolia include decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to promote skin health and treat conditions such as vitiligo and psoriasis.
